skill.name: designing-and-implementing
skill.description: Guides agents through requirement clarification, design options, task planning, and implementation for complex feature work.
skill.summary: Plan complex software changes before implementation using PairCoder planning commands and task workflows.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution, but the file contains documentation examples, not executable Ruby code. The external command risk is still relevant because the skill asks agents to run bpsai-pair commands and sync Trello, so users should review command effects before execution. Weak cryptography alerts on lines 2, 3, and 6 are false positives from plain text metadata and headings.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Require Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run bpsai-pair commands for intent checks, plan creation, Trello sync, and task updates. These commands appear relevant to the skill purpose, but they can modify project planning files or external task boards depending on the local CLI configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible and tied to the documented workflow. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because the commands are not hidden and match the skill description.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated Markdown code fences and inline command examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation, and no Ruby code or dynamic shell execution syntax is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are Markdown fences, inline paths, or inline command examples. There is no executable Ruby file or shell interpolation context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain front matter and a workflow heading, not cryptographic code. I found no hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or password storage behavior in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain text metadata and a Markdown heading. There is no evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or another weak cryptographic operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples for bpsai-pair. These are transparent workflow commands, but marketplace users should know the skill expects local CLI execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is confirmed as command guidance rather than hidden execution. The impact depends on the user's installed bpsai-pair CLI and project configuration.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering requests can fail when implementation starts before scope and design are clear. This skill provides a structured design, planning, task creation, and implementation workflow.
actual_capabilities: Helps decide when a request needs formal planning before implementation.
Guides requirement clarification and affected component discovery.
Prompts comparison of two or three design approaches with tradeoffs.
Defines a plan and task workflow using PairCoder files and commands.
Includes Trello sync guidance for teams using PairCoder integrations.
limitations: Requires the bpsai-pair CLI for the documented command workflow.
Does not implement features by itself without a connected coding agent.
Assumes PairCoder directory conventions such as .paircoder/plans and .paircoder/tasks.
Trello sync depends on local credentials and external service configuration.

use_cases.title: Scope a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a broad feature request into clarified requirements, design options, and implementation tasks.

use_cases.title: Plan a Refactor
use_cases.description: Compare refactor approaches before touching shared code or changing architecture.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Team Work
use_cases.description: Create PairCoder tasks and sync planned work to Trello for team visibility.

prompt_templates.title: Decide Whether to Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this request and tell me whether it needs a design plan before coding. Explain the affected components and any clarifying questions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose two or three implementation approaches for this feature. Include tradeoffs, risks, and a recommended path.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PairCoder Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this approved approach into a PairCoder plan with task objectives, priorities, complexity, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Execute the Planned Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the existing PairCoder plan to pick the next task, update task status, implement with tests first, and report progress after each task.

output_examples.input: Add team-level billing controls to the dashboard.
output_examples.output: A planning response that identifies billing, permissions, dashboard UI, and tests as affected areas, then asks focused requirement questions.

output_examples.input: Refactor the notification system without breaking existing integrations.
output: A comparison of incremental, adapter-based, and rewrite approaches.
A recommended approach based on migration risk and test coverage.
A task list with acceptance criteria for each migration step.

output_examples.input: Sync the approved plan to Trello and start the first task.
output_examples.output: A concise execution update that lists the planned sync action, selected first task, status change, and next implementation step.

best_practices: Use planning for features, refactors, and changes that affect multiple components.
Review bpsai-pair commands before execution in repositories with external integrations.
Keep acceptance criteria specific enough to verify with tests or visible behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not use this workflow for small typo fixes or simple documentation edits.
Do not sync plans to Trello until task scope and ownership are clear.
Do not skip design tradeoffs when changes affect shared architecture.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents move from feature requests to design options, plans, tasks, and implementation steps.

faq.question: Does it require PairCoder?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands and file locations assume the bpsai-pair CLI and PairCoder project structure.

faq.question: Can it be used without Trello?
faq.answer: Yes. The Trello sync step can be skipped when a project does not use that integration.

faq.question: Is it suitable for quick fixes?
faq.answer: Usually no. The skill explicitly recommends skipping planning for typo fixes, small bugs, and documentation tweaks.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should review local bpsai-pair configuration, target plan IDs, task IDs, and any enabled Trello credentials.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
